modules.title: Audio, DSP, and realtime safety
guarantees: DSP helpers produce correct observable output across realistic buffer, sample-rate, channel, and parameter boundaries.
Realtime paths avoid allocation and blocking behavior where that is a documented contract.
Offline render helpers and audio observability tools report stable metrics instead of only proving that code ran.
missing_or_weak: Needs systematic mutation proof for representative DSP and realtime invariants.
Needs clearer separation between unit, golden, performance, and realtime-safety tiers.
Host/DAW interaction remains partly outside the normal PR confidence signal.

modules.title: MIDI, MPE, UMP, and sysex
guarantees: MIDI parsers and buffers handle malformed, partial, realtime, sysex, MPE, and UMP inputs without corrupting event order.
Per-note expression and conversion paths preserve timing and channel semantics.
Platform MIDI backends fail or skip honestly when platform capability is absent.
missing_or_weak: Some platform MIDI smokes still need stronger observable assertions.
Mutation proof should cover parser abort/restart and per-note state invariants.

modules.title: State, parameters, presets, and edit history
guarantees: Parameter metadata, normalization, binding, host sync, and modulation-visible state round-trip correctly.
State trees, properties, presets, and edit history reject malformed data and preserve compatible serialized form.
User-visible state diagnostics identify bad restore inputs rather than silently succeeding.
missing_or_weak: Needs cross-version state fixture coverage for important serialized formats.
Adapter lifecycle state restore paths need stronger representative mutation proof.

modules.title: Format adapters, hosting, plugin scanning, and validation
guarantees: VST3, AU, AUv3, CLAP, LV2, ARA, AAX, and standalone adapters preserve lifecycle, bus, parameter, state, MIDI, latency, and editor contracts.
Host scanning, plugin slots, signal graphs, and real-plugin runners expose actionable failures instead of silent optional passes.
Validator output is captured and routed to owners when adapter compatibility regresses.
missing_or_weak: Validator cadence is still weak: manual or path-filtered lanes do not provide broad required PR confidence.
Real plugin cache and host integration tests need clearer required-vs-optional preflight semantics.
Mutation proof should cover adapter state, parameter, bus, and lifecycle invariants.

modules.title: Graph scheduling, anticipation, and render partitioning
guarantees: Graph anticipation classifies live, latent, feedback, sidechain, MIDI, and malformed graph edges without treating ineligible live paths as render-ahead work.
Partitioning and subgraph extraction preserve boundary ports and reject malformed or duplicate graph topology.

Anticipation render lanes produce boundary audio that matches synchronous rendering, handle underrun deterministically, avoid audio-thread allocation, and run race-free across producer/consumer paths.
missing_or_weak: Needs mutation proof for eligibility classification, boundary extraction, underrun behavior, and no-allocation guarantees.
Needs a clear tier split between fast graph-scheduling unit tests and longer race/stress coverage.

modules.title: View, UI, WebCompat, design import, and rendering
guarantees: View/widget behavior, layout, accessibility, text, rendering, and bridge APIs match documented user-visible semantics.
WebCompat and design import parsers reject malformed inputs and map supported source concepts to stable runtime output.
Visual/golden evidence is reviewed and separated from ordinary unit confidence.
missing_or_weak: Broad WebCompat/design-import coverage needs clearer parser, security, bridge integration, and visual-render subtiers.
GPU/font/screenshot capability labels are not yet consistently applied.
Placeholder visual/golden scaffolds should not count as normal passes.

modules.title: Runtime, events, IPC, networking, OSC, and security utilities
guarantees: Runtime primitives, result/error handling, crypto, XML/ZIP, URL, SIMD, and filesystem helpers fail deterministically and safely.
Event loops, async streams, IPC, network streams, message channels, OSC, and service discovery tear down deterministically.
Security-sensitive helpers reject malformed input and keep platform-dependent skips explicit.
missing_or_weak: Async/network tests need continued scrutiny for timing and environment assumptions.
Platform/device-dependent soft skips need stronger reporting and capability labels.

modules.title: CLI, tools, package workflows, local CI, and developer automation
guarantees: CLI commands return correct user-visible output, diagnostics, exit behavior, and artifact paths.
Shellout tests distinguish missing optional tools from missing required built binaries.
Local CI, package, content, kit, and maintenance scripts produce trustworthy automation evidence.
missing_or_weak: Shellout preflights still need stricter required-binary semantics.
Some CLI tests validate help text or process exit without enough product outcome assertions.

modules.title: Platform, Apple, Android, Windows, Linux, and device surfaces
guarantees: Platform-specific audio, MIDI, rendering, filesystem, permission, and package surfaces report correct behavior on supported hosts.
iOS/AUv3, Android, Windows, Linux, and macOS-only behavior is routed to explicit platform lanes instead of hidden in generic pass counts.
Device, SDK, GPU, display, audio-device, and validator dependencies are represented as capabilities.
missing_or_weak: PR/nightly/release obligations are still uneven across platforms.
Capability labels for SDK/device/display/GPU requirements are not yet consistently enforced.
